content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Both our features touch Option in core.py so full file-disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: I only need core.py (dynamic-default detection in Option.__init__/get_help_record + a second-pass resolution step in Command.main/parse_args). I don't need types.py at all - it's yours exclusively for batch_size/type coercion. For core.py, let's sequence instead of parallel-edit: I'll go first since my changes are small and localized (Option.__init__, one help-text branch, one block in Command.main), send DONE when finished, then you layer your batch_size changes on top. Sound OK?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/types.py
note: Accepted. Confirmed via reading the code: Option.get_default() already delegates to super().get_default() (Parameter.get_default, base class) for the non-multi-flag case, so I can implement dynamic-default resolution by editing: Parameter.__init__ (validation), Parameter.get_default (base, ~line 2232), Command.parse_args (second-pass resolution, ~line 1189-1210), and Option.get_help_extra (~line 2822, add a dict branch next to the existing 'inspect.isfunction -> (dynamic)' case) for the '[default: (dynamic)]' help text. I will NOT touch Option.__init__ or Option.to_info_dict. I don't need types.py at all - convert_type() already falls back to STRING when it can't classify a guessed dict default, no crash, so it's untouched. Starting now, will send DONE when finished.
